channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bangkok to review quake safety after collapse of high-rise leaves dozens missing
item.description: The governor of Bangkok says authorities are investigating why a high-rise under construction collapsed during an earthquake Friday that otherwise caused limited damage in the Thai capital.
item.dc:creator: Jerry Harmer, Jintamas Saksornchai And Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US armored vehicle missing in Lithuania pulled from swamp but fate of 4 soldiers is still unknown
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — A U.S. armored vehicle that went missing in Lithuania has been retrieved from a swamp after a six-day search but there is still no information about the fate of the four American soldiers who were on board, Lithuanian officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military orders evacuation of most of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: The Israeli military has issued sweeping evacuation orders covering most of Rafah, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: French court to rule in far right embezzlement case. Marine Le Pen's political future is at stake
item.description: A Paris court is expected to deliver a verdict in an embezzlement case that could shake up French politics and derail far-right leader Marine Le Pen’s career.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's Netanyahu nominates a new domestic security chief
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has nominated a new domestic security chief, after he moved to fire the current one over a crisis of confidence that critics say was politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Greenland prime minister says US will not get Greenland
item.description: The prime minister of Greenland pushed back Sunday against assertions by U.S. President Donald Trump that America will take control of the island territory. Greenland, a huge, resource-rich island in the Atlantic, is a self-governing territory of Denmark, a NATO ally of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian attack on hospital, shopping center leaves 2 dead as Ukraine braces for fresh offensive
item.description: A Russian drone attack killed two people in Kharkiv on Sunday amid a fresh offensive ahead of peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landmark UK report on Hamas massacre exposes worst attack on Jews since Holocaust
item.description: A UK Parliament report chaired by respected historian Lord Andrew Roberts calls the Oct. 7 Hamas attack in Israel deliberate and brutal, urging democracies to combat denial and misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump finds fault with both Putin and Zelenskyy as he tries to push for deal to end war in Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump has lashed out at both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, expressing frustration with the Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he struggles to forge a truce to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With federal election looming, Liberal Canadian PM accused of plagiarism while studying at Oxford: report
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is embroiled in controversy after a Canadian news outlet discovered at least 10 instances of possible plagiarism in his 1995 Oxford thesis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump golfs with Finland's president amid icy relationship with EU
item.description: President Trump joined the leader of Finland, a member of the European Union (EU), for a round of golf Saturday, despite trade tensions with the executive body, which recently announced levies on agricultural products and clothing items imported from the United States slated to take effect in mid-Ap
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syria swears in new transitional government 4 months after Assad's removal
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new transitional government was sworn in Saturday nearly four months after the Assad family was removed from power and as the new authorities in Damascus work to bring back stability to the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it accepts a new Gaza ceasefire proposal but Israel makes a counter-offer
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Hamas militant group said Saturday it has accepted a new Gaza ceasefire proposal from mediators Egypt and Qatar, but Israel said it has made a counter-proposal in “full coordination” with the third mediator, the United States.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s denial of Mexico’s Colorado River request sparks concerns over future water negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration’s unprecedented decision to deny a delivery of water to Mexico is raising alarm among experts, who fear it could jeopardize future cross-border negotiations in an increasingly thirsty region.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, The Hill

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Burma, Thailand earthquake rises to more than 1,600 victims
item.description: The death toll from the devastating earthquake that rattled Burma and Thailand on Friday has risen sharply to more than 1,650 victims. Nearly 140 people still missing, Burma's ruling military said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kalshi CEO: Political betting could be ‘a trillion dollar’ market
item.description: Kalshi says it’s the first regulated exchange where people “can buy and sell contracts on the outcome of events.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, The Hill

item.title: Nostalgia, relief and loss as some Syrians mark their first Ramadan back home in years
item.description: DARAYA, Syria (AP) — When Mariam Aabour learned of the ouster of Syrian leader Bashar Assad, she shed tears of joy. But as the time came to return to her homeland from Lebanon – where she fled years earlier – Aabour felt torn.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Kingdom could be only G7 nation not to produce its own steel; Chinese owner blames Trump tariffs
item.description: A Chinese steel group that owns British Steel is blaming U.S. tariffs for the potential closure of two blast furnaces that could impact the steel industry in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Denmark in Trump's crosshairs as Vance makes ambitious appeal to Greenland
item.description: Vice President Vance looked to appeal to Greenland by urging it to side with the US, not Russia or China, after it seeks independence from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration moves to eliminate USAID, firing remaining employees
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to formally end the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), notifying the remaining employees they will be terminated and the agency will be merged with the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 1,000
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 1,000 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation
